files.00_Overview.md: Add Learning Objectives section at the beginning. Add Key Takeaways section before Concept Check. Expand Quick Reference table with more predefined dimensions (dimVolume, dimArea, dimTime, etc.). The file is already well-structured and concise.
files.01_Introduction.md: MAJOR OVERHAUL: This file is extremely long and verbose compared to others. It contains excessive Thai text with detailed explanations, mermaid diagrams, image placeholders with DALL-E prompts, and extensive source code references that should be simplified. Remove or condense the Thai bridge-building analogy introduction. Remove mermaid flowchart - it's redundant with text. Remove all `![[...]]` image placeholder blocks with DALL-E prompts. Condense the Navier-Stokes dimensional analysis section - it's too detailed for an intro. The 3W framework is not applied - there's no clear What/Why/How structure. Add Learning Objectives at start. This file should be 1/3 its current length.
files.02_DimensionSet_Advanced.md: Add Learning Objectives section. Add Key Takeaways before Concept Check. Content is well-structured and concise - good example to follow. Consider adding more examples of creating custom dimensions for thermal/mass transfer applications.
files.03_Dimension_Arithmetic.md: Add Learning Objectives section. Add Key Takeaways before Concept Check. Content is well-structured. Good examples. Consider adding more complex examples with fvc/fvm operations showing dimension propagation through differential operators.
files.04_Non_Dimensionalization.md: Add Learning Objectives section. Add Key Takeaways before Concept Check. Content is good but could benefit from more practical OpenFOAM examples - when would someone actually need to non-dimensionalize in practice? Add a section on 'When to Use Non-Dimensionalization' with real CFD workflow examples.
files.05_Advanced_Applications.md: Add Learning Objectives section. Add Key Takeaways before Concept Check. Content is well-structured but the turbulence quantities section could be expanded with more k-epsilon, k-omega, Spalart-Allmaras examples. The Non-Dimensional Groups section duplicates some content from file 04 - consider consolidating or adding cross-reference.
files.06_Common_Pitfalls.md: Add Learning Objectives section. Good structure with Problem/Solution format. Could add more pitfalls around: dimension propagation in fvc/fvm operations, incorrect dimension specifications in boundary conditions, mixing dimensionedScalar with plain scalar in conditional statements. Add Key Takeaways section.
files.07_Summary_and_Exercises.md: Add Learning Objectives section (even though it's summary, learners should know what practicing these exercises will achieve). The 'Summary' section is too brief - expand with more comprehensive key concepts matrix. Exercises are good but could be more challenging - add a 'Challenge Exercise' that requires combining multiple concepts. Consider adding 'Project Idea' section suggesting a mini-implementation project.

global_strategy: This folder has INCONSISTENT QUALITY across files. File 01 is massively over-developed with excessive Thai content, mermaid diagrams, and image placeholders, while files 02-07 are concise and well-structured. GLOBAL REFACTORING APPROACH: 1) Apply 3W Framework (What/Why/How) to ALL files - currently only partial application, 2) Add Learning Objectives to ALL files at the very beginning, 3) Add Key Takeaways to ALL files before the Concept Check section, 4) Remove ALL image placeholder blocks with DALL-E prompts (![[...]]), 5) Reduce Thai language content in 01_Introduction.md by 70% - it should match the concise style of files 02-07, 6) Remove mermaid diagrams from 01_Introduction.md - they're redundant, 7) Eliminate redundancy between 04_Non_Dimensionalization.md and 05_Advanced_Applications.md (Non-Dimensional Groups appear in both), 8) Standardize section headers across all files using consistent capitalization and numbering. TARGET: Each file should be 100-200 lines maximum, with consistent structure: Learning Objectives → Overview/What → Why → How → Examples → Quick Reference → Key Takeaways → Concept Check → Related Docs.
